gpt_record: EDC/HOBt procedure text recorded as compound for mp 90-91
adjudication: Claude compound identity is correct; GPT captured procedural prose instead of the compound heading.
root_cause: Nearest-preceding-text heuristic crossed from compound name into method prose.
recommendation: Snap evidence windows to compound-heading/paragraph boundaries and require a chemical-name-shaped string before accepting a name.

gpt_record: Two records for compound 29 at 227-229, one with typo A-adamantan
claude_record: One record for compound 29 at midpoint 228
adjudication: One compound/value should be retained; GPT duplicate is incorrect.
root_cause: Duplicate matching failed because one name variant carried OCR/text typo.
recommendation: Deduplicate by article + compound label + normalized chemical name + normalized temperature.

adjudication: If open bounds are in scope, GPT is correct to retain >270 with qualifier; Claude missed it. If only exact/range values are in scope, skipping is acceptable.
root_cause: No explicit policy for open-bounded temperatures.
recommendation: Add temperature_relation column (=, range, >, <) and retain open bounds when requested.

adjudication: Claude/GPT source value is correct, but GPT name is a section title and is wrong.
root_cause: Fallback to section heading when paragraph name resolution failed.
recommendation: Reject section titles/ranges as compound names unless also a complete compound identity.

adjudication: Claude is correct for the 144-146 value; GPT assigned it to B instead of precursor A. B is 178-179.
root_cause: Nearby intermediate A/B names are similar and the parser collapsed them.
recommendation: Track local labels A/B exactly and verify against the immediate sentence containing the mp.

adjudication: Open-bound values are real article data; retain with qualifier if open bounds are in scope.
root_cause: Claude policy skipped open bounds.
recommendation: Represent open bounds structurally rather than forcing a numeric-only field.

adjudication: GPT has the more useful standalone name; Claude row is not standalone.
root_cause: Claude could not resolve local compound 3 from surrounding text.
recommendation: Resolve compound labels from preceding synthesis context or skip.

gpt_record: Duplicate ligand records for compounds 4 and 6
adjudication: Claude is correct to keep one row per compound/value.
root_cause: GPT merged table and inline extraction without duplicate suppression.
recommendation: Deduplicate across table and prose sources with same compound/value/property.

claude_rows: Claude rows 7-9 plus skipped open bound
claude_record: Metal complex names include ligand identity for 8/9/11; complex 10 >300 skipped
adjudication: Claude names are better for 8/9/11; GPT names are not standalone. GPT additionally captured >300 for complex 10, which should be retained only if open bounds are in scope and ligand identity is resolved.
root_cause: GPT did not cross-reference ligand labels 4/6 before naming complexes.
recommendation: For complexes, resolve ligand name and metal identity; do not emit local label alone.

adjudication: Open-bound values are real article data; retain with qualifier if open bounds are in scope.
root_cause: Claude policy skipped open bounds.
recommendation: Add relation-qualified temperature fields.

adjudication: Claude is correct to skip; source footnote says this is reference mp for compound 1a, already represented.
root_cause: GPT interpreted a series heading/reference note as a compound-specific row.
recommendation: Reject plural/range headings (2a-g) as compound names and tie footnotes to their referenced row.

gpt_record: Names contain X= and R= substituent fields without scaffold attachment positions
adjudication: Both tables are insufficient for standalone use unless the X/R positions can be resolved from the scaffold/scheme; values are table-correct.
root_cause: Both extractors preserved table variables instead of resolving structures.
recommendation: Require R-group table resolution to a full name/structure; otherwise mark unresolved and skip from final CSV.

gpt_record: Table 5 STRM/SIRM predicted Tb/Tm and literature Tm values for APIs
adjudication: Source contains per-compound Tb/Tm values. If calculated/literature values are in scope, GPT is closer but must keep method/data_origin fields; if only article-measured values are in scope, Claude skip is correct.
root_cause: Ambiguous inclusion policy for predicted and literature-cited data.
recommendation: Separate data_origin: measured_by_article, literature_cited, predicted_calculated; filter by user policy.

adjudication: These two GPT rows are wrong names; source identifies Dexamethasone and Thalidomide literature Tm values.
root_cause: Parser used section heading for narrative comparison values.
recommendation: Blacklist generic section titles and require compound co-occurrence near the value.

gpt_record: Prose fragments The minor components/The precursors
adjudication: Claude names are correct for shared values; GPT names are wrong.
root_cause: GPT used paragraph headings/prose fragments as names.
recommendation: Reject prose headings and resolve named compound before colon/property list.

adjudication: Claude captured an additional literature mp for the same compound; GPT omitted it.
root_cause: GPT captured only first value from a semicolon-separated pair.
recommendation: Handle multiple values in one evidence span as separate provenance-qualified rows.

adjudication: GPT DOI is correct; Claude failed DOI resolution.
root_cause: Claude stopped at missing local metadata instead of external/metadata DOI lookup.
recommendation: Require DOI field to be DOI; if not in local metadata, verify from article landing page or Crossref when network is allowed.

gpt_record: Inclusion compound names for host melting
claude_record: Dehydrocholic acid host in named clathrate
adjudication: Claude is more precise: the table column is host melting Tm in each clathrate environment, not a melting point of the guest.
root_cause: GPT over-read clathrate row label as compound name.
recommendation: Use multi-row table headers to bind property target, not just row label.

adjudication: Claude is correct; GPT name is section heading.
root_cause: Section heading fallback.
recommendation: Reject class headings as compound names.

gpt_record: Chlorine dioxide duplicated with case variants
adjudication: Claude is correct; GPT duplicates are incorrect.
root_cause: Table plus prose/case-insensitive duplicate not merged.
recommendation: Deduplicate case-insensitively by normalized name/property/value/source.

gpt_record: No pure glutaraldehyde mp/bp, Bronopol mp, BHAP bp, MBT mp
claude_record: Claude extracted these named biocide property values
adjudication: Claude is more complete for this article; values are in narrative/table property text.
root_cause: GPT search windows missed some narrative property statements.
recommendation: Use exhaustive property-pattern scan plus entity resolution for each hit.

adjudication: Claude is more precise: 100.5 C refers to the 50% aqueous solution, not pure glutaraldehyde.
root_cause: GPT dropped concentration qualifier from compound/material name.
recommendation: Preserve formulation/concentration when the value is for a mixture or solution.

gpt_record: Full names for 4h-4j plus duplicate generic benzoxanthenone derivative 4h-4j; generic 4a-4g
claude_record: Claude has 10 rows, better 4a and full 4h-4j, but 4b-4g still generic
adjudication: Claude is better overall. GPT duplicate 4h-4j generic rows are incorrect. Both 4b-4g names are not standalone if final table requires full identities.
root_cause: GPT combined table rows and inline characterization without merging; both lacked structure resolution for known derivatives.
recommendation: Merge table and characterization rows by product code; resolve product codes from schemes/structures or skip unresolved known compounds.

claude_record: Experimental bp and mp columns for 100-compound test set; 100 bp + 96 mp values
adjudication: Claude is correct to extract the experimental bp/mp values; GPT missed an entire PDF table.
root_cause: GPT apparently treated the QSPR review as aggregate/prediction-only and did not inspect Table 2.
recommendation: Even in modeling/review papers, scan tables for per-compound experimental property columns before deciding zero rows.

claude_record: Claude extracted experimental columns only, not predicted software columns
adjudication: If predicted values are in scope, both tables are incomplete. If final dataset is for measured/literature physical constants, Claude policy is correct.
root_cause: Ambiguous predicted-vs-experimental inclusion policy.
recommendation: Use data_origin and method columns; let skill mode determine whether predictions are emitted.

gpt_record: GPT includes calculated Eq.16 values and first-cycle/peak/onset DSC events
claude_record: Claude keeps curated experimental Tfus values and literature experimental values
adjudication: For a measured-constants table, Claude is more appropriate. GPT values are not numerically wrong but mix calculated values and auxiliary DSC events into the same output.
root_cause: No policy for calculated values and DSC onset/peak/first-cycle events.
recommendation: Represent thermal_event and data_origin explicitly; choose paper-defined Tfus unless user asks for all DSC event values.

adjudication: Claude captured one experimental salt not present in GPT.
root_cause: GPT missed one table/literature row.
recommendation: Run per-table row-count reconciliation against source table.

gpt_record: Ranges/open bounds stored as strings in temperature_c
claude_record: Ranges collapsed to midpoints in value_celsius with original value often first endpoint
adjudication: Neither schema is ideal alone. Preserve exact original string plus normalized numeric fields and relation/range endpoints.
root_cause: Different unspoken normalization assumptions.
recommendation: Use value_original_text, value_c_min, value_c_max, value_c_midpoint, relation, unit_original.

gpt_record: Many names include local labels/citations like (5e), [36], Method 2, compound N
claude_record: Same issue, generally somewhat cleaner but still present
adjudication: These should be removed from final compound_name and stored separately if useful.
root_cause: Compound identity and article-local provenance were conflated.
recommendation: Separate compound_name from compound_label, method_label, citation, and evidence_text.
